content: Just as the human brain consolidates only high-surprise, curiosity-prioritized memory traces via active memory selection, artificial curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (RL) agents should integrate an active human-like memory gating mechanism to discard trivial predictable surprises, rather than rewarding all prediction errors generated by intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) or random network distillation (RND). This resolves a longstanding flaw where curious RL agents get trapped in noisy, high-entropy environments by aligning intrinsic curiosity to the same adaptive filtering the brain uses for efficient learning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
